title: Quiz: Vision for the Quantum Internet

questions.question: What is the ultimate goal of the quantum internet?

options.text: Any-to-any high-fidelity entanglement on demand worldwide

questions.explanation: The quantum internet aims to enable any two nodes worldwide to share high-fidelity entanglement on demand, enabling fundamentally new capabilities like distributed quantum computing and unconditionally secure communication.

questions.question: According to the Wehner-Elkouss-Hanson framework, which stage enables device-independent QKD?
questions.explanation: Device-independent QKD requires heralded entanglement distribution (Stage 2), as security is proven through Bell inequality violations of the shared entanglement rather than trusting device behavior.

questions.question: What is distributed quantum computing?

options.text: Having quantum computers in different buildings
Linking quantum processors via entanglement for combined computational power

questions.explanation: Distributed quantum computing links multiple quantum processors via entanglement, allowing them to work together as one larger quantum computer. This enables exponential scaling without building ever-larger single processors.

questions.question: What role do satellites play in the global quantum internet architecture?
questions.explanation: Satellites provide intercontinental and ocean-crossing quantum links where fiber is impractical. They form the highest tier of the hierarchical global architecture, connecting continental backbone networks.

questions.question: What is a quantum-enhanced telescope array?

options.text: Multiple telescopes linked by entanglement for unprecedented resolution
A telescope that detects quantum particles

questions.explanation: A quantum-enhanced telescope array uses entanglement to link multiple telescopes across large baselines, achieving resolution impossible with classical interferometry. This could enable revolutionary astronomical observations.

questions.question: When is the full quantum internet (Stage 6) expected?
questions.explanation: The full quantum internet with universal quantum nodes and complete capabilities is estimated for 2050 or beyond, requiring advances in quantum computing, long-lived memories, and global infrastructure.

questions.question: Quantum memory networks are needed for asynchronous quantum operations across time zones.
questions.explanation: True. Without quantum memories, quantum operations must be synchronized in real-time. Memories allow storing quantum states until all parties are ready, enabling asynchronous protocols across different time zones.
